goal: Consolidate user-token issuance in auth-proxy and make the backend a pure resource server.
notes: Brownfield test convention: this repo uses co-located vitest suites, NOT pytest-bdd .feature files. Acceptance/integration coverage is expressed as auth-proxy/*.test.ts (full Hono app, real keypair) + frontend unit tests, per design.md §Stage 2 ('Prefer the LIGHTEST test level').
Stage 1 (issuance moved to auth-proxy) has ALREADY landed — see auth-proxy/user-token-issuance.test.ts (all 20 rows green) and lib/user-token.ts.
THIS RUN implements Stage 2 ONLY. Stage 3 steps are recorded for completeness but are explicitly out of scope and unstarted.
Driving ports for Stage 2: (P1) auth-proxy HTTP ingress `POST /api/orgs` (full Hono app); (P2) frontend `withAuth(fetchFn)` response interceptor.

stages.title: Response-header reissue on org-create
stages.risk: low (additive; existing flows continue to work)
exit_criteria: auth-proxy observes POST /api/orgs returning 201 and sets X-New-Access-Token + X-New-Token-Expires-In on the response, minted via the existing getKeypair()/jose user-token path with the new org_id claim.
auth-proxy strips any inbound X-New-Access-Token / X-New-Token-Expires-In on upstream responses so only auth-proxy's own injection survives (R7).
frontend withAuth consumes X-New-Access-Token + X-New-Token-Expires-In from every response and calls setToken + setTokenExpiry.
X-New-Access-Token (+ expires header) documented as sensitive in auth-proxy/README.md (R6).
backend untouched; ui-state onboarding retry loop still calls /api/auth/reissue and keeps working (independently deployable).

steps.description: Org-create reissue hook: pure module that decides whether to reissue (POST /api/orgs + 201 + org_id in body) and mints a fresh user token preserving sub/email/sid/name with the new org_id.
acceptance: Fires on POST /api/orgs 201 with an org id in the body; produces X-New-Access-Token + X-New-Token-Expires-In.
Does NOT fire on non-201, on GET /api/orgs, or on other paths (POST /api/projects).
Extracts org_id from flat ({id|org_id}) and JSON:API ({data.id}) body shapes.
Preserves sub/email/sid; only org_id changes; expires-in matches the token TTL.
Anonymous / non-user-token caller (no preservable sid): hook does NOT fire.

steps.description: Symmetric outbound strip (R7): drop any upstream-supplied X-New-Access-Token / X-New-Token-Expires-In before relaying, mirroring the inbound identity-header strip. Only auth-proxy's own mint can reach the FE.
acceptance: Backend-supplied X-New-Access-Token on a 200 POST /api/projects is stripped (row 8).
Smuggled header on a 201 from a non-org path is stripped and the hook does not fire (row 9).
Smuggled header on POST /api/orgs 201 is replaced — only auth-proxy's keypair-verifiable mint survives (row 10).

steps.description: Frontend consumes the reissue: withAuth reads X-New-Access-Token + X-New-Token-Expires-In from every response and, when present and non-empty, calls setToken + setTokenExpiry (expiry = now + expires_in*1000). Reuses existing tokenStorage primitives; no new flow.
acceptance: Response carrying X-New-Access-Token updates the stored token and expiry.
Response without the header leaves stored token untouched.
Reissue consumption does not interfere with the existing 401-refresh-replay path.

steps.description: Document X-New-Access-Token (+ X-New-Token-Expires-In) as sensitive in auth-proxy/README.md alongside Authorization; operators must apply the same header-logging redaction (R6).
acceptance: README names the headers as sensitive with the R6 redaction note.

stages.title: Dissolve the onboarding retry loop and delete the dead path
stages.scope: OUT OF SCOPE FOR THIS RUN — follow-up MR(s)
stages.risk: high (deletes ~600 lines), but no callers remain after stages 1-2
exit_criteria: ui-state creating_org → error_recoverable → error_terminal subgraph removed; reissueOrgJwtFn deleted; getOrgAndReissue becomes getOrg.
backend /api/auth/* endpoints deleted; direct-JWT-verification middleware branch removed; dev_provider/workos_provider/dev_keys/rate_limiter deleted.

steps.description: ui-state: dissolve the onboarding retry loop (states, guards, reissue actor, retry budgets).
backend: delete /api/auth/* router, providers, dev keys, rate limiter; remove the direct-JWT-verification middleware branch; remove the FE /api/auth/reissue call.
